message:
feat: add perf topdown/c2c profiling commands, harden freelist serialization, and expand E2E coverage

Pager (fsqlite-pager):
- Fix freelist serialization to use committed_db_size (max of inner.db_size
  and highest written page) instead of raw inner.db_size, preventing
  beyond-db-size freelist entries from being included in commit predictions
  for concurrent transactions that allocate new pages
- Add committed_db_size_with_inner() helper to SimpleTransaction for
  consistent db_size calculation across commit prediction and serialization
- Filter predicted commit pages against committed_db_size upper bound

E2E (fsqlite-e2e):
- Add format_hot_path_perf_topdown_command() with automatic fallback from
  TopdownL1 metrics to core event counters when perf stat -M TopdownL1
  is unsupported on the host
- Add format_hot_path_perf_c2c_command() for cache-to-cache contention
  profiling with raw data capture
- Add format_hot_path_bash_command() and hot_path_sidecar_relpath() helpers

Other crate fixes across btree balance, compat persistence, FTS5, MVCC,
and planner codegen reflecting concurrent agent development.

Also updates beads issue tracker metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-db300.1.2","title":"A2: freeze the canonical Beads fixture and workload matrix","description":"## Goal
Freeze the canonical benchmark matrix for this optimization campaign using the Beads databases already in `/dp` and the repo's working benchmark area. The matrix must make it impossible to accidentally compare different fixtures, workloads, retry policies, build profiles, placement profiles, or hardware classes when claiming wins.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The matrix explicitly names fixture source paths plus immutable fixture identifiers or digests, workloads, concurrency levels, modes, retry policy, build profile, seed policy, placement profile, and hardware-class identifier.
2. SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs are all represented, with baseline and recommended pinned placement profiles always included and an adversarial topology profile included where relevant or available.
3. Output locations and naming conventions are stable enough for mechanical diffing over time and can be linked back to the source revision and Beads data hash used to produce them.
4. The matrix is explicit enough that later scorecard and verification beads can distinguish a transferable win from a lucky one-machine result without reinterpreting raw logs by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The matrix explicitly names fixture source paths plus immutable fixture identifiers or digests, workloads, concurrency levels, modes, retry policy, build profile, seed policy, placement profile, and hardware-class identifier.
2. SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs are all represented, with baseline and recommended pinned placement profiles always included and an adversarial topology profile included where relevant or available.
3. Output locations and naming conventions are stable enough for mechanical diffing over time and can be linked back to the source revision and Beads data hash used to produce them.
4. The matrix is explicit enough that later scorecard and verification beads can distinguish a transferable win from a lucky one-machine result without reinterpreting raw logs by hand.","notes":"This should cover the real Beads fixtures already in use, including the `frankensqlite`, `frankentui`, and `frankensearch` databases, plus the challenging workloads already proven useful: `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys`, `hot_page_contention`, and `mixed_read_write`, augmented with the placement profiles needed for honest many-core comparisons.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureCedar","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:51:52.803754130Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:10.654322029Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T18:28:35.115825222Z","close_reason":"Completed: canonical Beads benchmark matrix/schema/artifact contract verified in the live tree via rch-targeted test and cargo-check passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","fixtures","measurement","perf","realdb","track-a"],"comments":[{"id":2211,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.2","author":"AzureCedar","text":"Collapsed the duplicate Beads benchmark campaign compile surface back onto crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fixture_select.rs so there is one canonical contract again. Aligned the checked-in JSON schema and RUNNING_THE_E2E_SUITE.md with the real manifest, added real-manifest schema/artifact-path tests, and kept the bead claimed/in_progress. Verification: rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets passed; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_bd_db300_1_2_lib cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e --lib beads_benchmark_campaign -- --nocapture passed (5/5). cargo fmt --check is still red because unrelated dirty files already exist in connection.rs/perf_runner.rs/fixture_select.rs formatting, and targeted clippy still fails in unrelated crates/fsqlite-wal/src/wal_fec.rs:1780 (clippy::needless_pass_by_ref_mut).","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:49:42Z"},{"id":2212,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.2","author":"BlackDove","text":"Verification pass on the already-landed A2 work:
- The canonical Beads benchmark campaign compile surface is consolidated in `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fixture_select.rs`, with `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/lib.rs` no longer exporting the stale duplicate module.
- The tracked schema at `sample_sqlite_db_files/manifests/beads_benchmark_campaign.v1.schema.json` matches the checked-in manifest shape, and the real-manifest artifact-path tests are exercised from the `fixture_select` test surface.
- Offloaded verification passed via `rch`:
  1. `rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e beads_benchmark_campaign -- --nocapture` => 5/5 relevant tests passed (`test_expand_beads_benchmark_campaign`, `test_validate_beads_benchmark_campaign_sample`, `test_load_beads_benchmark_campaign_real`, `test_beads_benchmark_campaign_manifest_matches_json_schema`, `test_real_beads_benchmark_campaign_bundle_path_contract`).
  2. `rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets` => exit 0.
- No code changes from BlackDove here; this is verification/unblock work on top of the existing tree state so downstream Track A/G beads can trust the matrix contract.","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:28:27Z"}]}

Updated the checked-in manifest schema/data and RUNNING_THE_E2E_SUITE.md to match. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fixture_select.rs; rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-e2e --all-targets (exit 0); rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e beads_benchmark_campaign -- --nocapture (6 targeted fixture_select tests passed). Note: rch cargo fmt --check surfaced unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in connection.rs, realdb_e2e.rs, perf_runner.rs, and eprocess.rs outside this bead surface.","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:44:37Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.1.3","title":"A3: automate perf, strace, hyperfine, and allocation artifact capture","description":"## Goal
Automate collection of the primary evidence artifacts for the hot benchmark cells: wall-clock benchmarks, sampled and off-CPU profiles, syscall summaries, allocation-oriented profiles, and topology-aware hardware-counter captures such as topdown, cache-to-cache/HITM, scheduler wakeup, and migration evidence when the host supports them. The purpose is to stop re-deriving ad hoc command lines every time a new hotspot investigation starts and to make cache, NUMA, and synchronization diagnosis first-class rather than afterthoughts.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. There is a repeatable workflow for `hyperfine`/`perf`/`strace`/heap-oriented collection on the canonical matrix, plus topology-aware `perf stat`/topdown and `perf c2c`/HITM or best-available equivalents on supported hosts.
2. Artifact names encode fixture, workload, concurrency, mode, date, and build profile.
3. Artifact manifests capture exact command lines, source revision, Beads data hash, tool versions, fixture identifier, kernel version, and placement policy for each run.
4. At least one representative hot cell is captured end-to-end through the automated flow in both profiler-safe and full-validation forms, including one cache-or-NUMA-focused evidence pack and one off-CPU-or-scheduler-focused evidence pack.
5. The workflow records when a requested counter set is unavailable and what fallback metric or trace was used instead.
6. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"1. There is a repeatable workflow for `hyperfine`/`perf`/`strace`/heap-oriented collection on the canonical matrix, plus topology-aware `perf stat`/topdown and `perf c2c`/HITM or best-available equivalents on supported hosts.
2. Artifact names encode fixture, workload, concurrency, mode, date, and build profile.
3. Artifact manifests capture exact command lines, source revision, Beads data hash, tool versions, fixture identifier, kernel version, and placement policy for each run.
4. At least one representative hot cell is captured end-to-end through the automated flow in both profiler-safe and full-validation forms, including one cache-or-NUMA-focused evidence pack and one off-CPU-or-scheduler-focused evidence pack.
5. The workflow records when a requested counter set is unavailable and what fallback metric or trace was used instead.
6. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"This bead should make it trivial for future work to compare before/after evidence on the exact same scenario. It is infrastructure for optimization, not an optional convenience, and it needs to support both "what burned CPU?" and "what caused wall-time delay on this topology?" questions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:52:03.071277420Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:55:28.741491489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","measurement","perf","profiling","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:43Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:43Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.1.4","title":"A4: define the performance scorecard and regression thresholds","description":"## Goal
Define the scorecard that this program will use to decide whether an optimization is real: throughput, retries, wall time, CPU utilization, IPC, futex share, p50/p95/p99 end-to-end latency, commit-latency markers, placement-profile stability, and any mode-specific indicators that matter. Without this, the project will keep drifting between numbers that are not directly comparable.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The scorecard lists every primary metric and its collection source.
2. Thresholds or target directions are explicit for throughput, retry pressure, blocking indicators, and `p50/p95/p99` end-to-end plus commit-latency markers.
3. The scorecard also defines how to treat placement-profile stability, including what counts as a transferable win versus a profile-specific lab result.
4. Where a claimed improvement relies on cache, NUMA, or scheduler behavior, the corresponding supporting indicators are named explicitly on the scorecard.
5. The scorecard is usable by later rollout and reporting beads without additional interpretation work.
6. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The scorecard lists every primary metric and its collection source.
2. Thresholds or target directions are explicit for throughput, retry pressure, blocking indicators, and `p50/p95/p99` end-to-end plus commit-latency markers.
3. The scorecard also defines how to treat placement-profile stability, including what counts as a transferable win versus a profile-specific lab result.
4. Where a claimed improvement relies on cache, NUMA, or scheduler behavior, the corresponding supporting indicators are named explicitly on the scorecard.
5. The scorecard is usable by later rollout and reporting beads without additional interpretation work.
6. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"Current evidence strongly suggests that CPU utilization and futex share belong on the scorecard alongside raw ops/s, because the project is underutilizing many-core hardware even when retry counts are acceptable. Users also care whether the win survives outside one carefully pinned benchmark profile.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:52:53.344575168Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:55:29.033145398Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","measurement","perf","scorecard","thresholds","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:44Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:44Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+fn format_hot_path_bash_command(output_dir: &Path, relpath: &str, script: String) -> String {
+    mkdir_prefixed_capture_command(
+        output_dir,
+        relpath,
+        format!(
+            "bash -lc {}",
+            shell_escape(&format!("set -euo pipefail; {script}"))
+        ),
+    )
+}
+
+fn hot_path_sidecar_relpath(relpath: &str, suffix: &str) -> String {
+    relpath.strip_suffix(".summary.txt").map_or_else(
+        || format!("{relpath}{suffix}"),
+        |prefix| format!("{prefix}{suffix}"),
+    )
+}
+
+fn format_hot_path_perf_topdown_command(
+    output_dir: &Path,
+    relpath: &str,
+    replay_command: &str,
+) -> String {
+    let output_path = hot_path_output_path(output_dir, relpath);
+    format_hot_path_bash_command(
+        output_dir,
+        relpath,
+        format!(
+            "out={}; tmp=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -f \"$tmp\"' EXIT; \
+             if perf stat -M TopdownL1 -o \"$tmp\" -- true >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
+                 printf '%s\\n' 'capture=topdown' 'selected=TopdownL1' 'fallback=false' > \"$out\"; \
+                 perf stat -x, -M TopdownL1 -o \"$tmp\" -- {replay_command}; \
+             else \
+                 printf '%s\\n' 'capture=topdown' 'selected=core_event_fallback' 'fallback=true' 'reason=TopdownL1 unsupported on this host' > \"$out\"; \
+                 perf stat -x, -e cycles,instructions,branches,branch-misses,cache-references,cache-misses -o \"$tmp\" -- {replay_command}; \
+             fi; \
+             cat \"$tmp\" >> \"$out\"",
+            shell_escape(&output_path),
+        ),
+    )
+}
+
+fn format_hot_path_perf_c2c_command(
+    output_dir: &Path,
+    relpath: &str,
+    replay_command: &str,
+) -> String {
+    let output_path = hot_path_output_path(output_dir, relpath);
+    let raw_path = hot_path_output_path(output_dir, &hot_path_sidecar_relpath(relpath, ".data"));
+    format_hot_path_bash_command(
+        output_dir,
+        relpath,
+        format!(
+            "out={}; raw={}; tmp=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -f \"$tmp\"' EXIT; \
+             rm -f \"$raw\"; \
+             if perf c2c record -o \"$raw\" -- {replay_command} >/dev/null 2>&1 && \
+                 perf c2c report --stats -i \"$raw\" > \"$tmp\" 2>&1; then \
+                 printf '%s\\n' 'capture=cache_to_cache' 'selected=perf_c2c' 'fallback=false' > \"$out\"; \
+                 cat \"$tmp\" >> \"$out\"; \
+             elif perf mem record -o \"$raw\" -- {replay_command} >/dev/null 2>&1 && \
+                 perf mem report -i \"$raw\" --stdio > \"$tmp\" 2>&1; then \
+                 printf '%s\\n' 'capture=cache_to_cache' 'selected=perf_mem_fallback' 'fallback=true' 'reason=perf c2c unavailable or failed on this host' > \"$out\"; \
+                 cat \"$tmp\" >> \"$out\"; \
+             else \
+                 rm -f \"$raw\"; \
+                 printf '%s\\n' 'capture=cache_to_cache' 'selected=cache_event_fallback' 'fallback=true' 'reason=perf c2c and perf mem unavailable or failed on this host' > \"$out\"; \
+                 perf stat -x, -e cache-references,cache-misses -o \"$tmp\" -- {replay_command}; \
+                 cat \"$tmp\" >> \"$out\"; \
+             fi",
+            shell_escape(&output_path),
+            shell_escape(&raw_path),
+        ),
+    )
+}
+
+fn format_hot_path_perf_migration_command(
+    output_dir: &Path,
+    relpath: &str,
+    replay_command: &str,
+) -> String {
+    let output_path = hot_path_output_path(output_dir, relpath);
+    format_hot_path_bash_command(
+        output_dir,
+        relpath,
+        format!(
+            "out={}; tmp=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -f \"$tmp\"' EXIT; \
+             if perf stat -e cpu-migrations,context-switches -o \"$tmp\" -- true >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
+                 printf '%s\\n' 'capture=migration' 'selected=cpu_migrations' 'fallback=false' > \"$out\"; \
+                 perf stat -x, -e cpu-migrations,context-switches -o \"$tmp\" -- {replay_command}; \
+             else \
+                 printf '%s\\n' 'capture=migration' 'selected=context_switch_fallback' 'fallback=true' 'reason=cpu-migrations unsupported on this host' > \"$out\"; \
+                 perf stat -x, -e context-switches,task-clock -o \"$tmp\" -- {replay_command}; \
+             fi; \
+             cat \"$tmp\" >> \"$out\"",
+            shell_escape(&output_path),
+        ),
+    )
+}
+
+fn format_hot_path_perf_mem_remote_access_command(
+    output_dir: &Path,
+    relpath: &str,
+    replay_command: &str,
+) -> String {
+    let output_path = hot_path_output_path(output_dir, relpath);
+    let raw_path = hot_path_output_path(output_dir, &hot_path_sidecar_relpath(relpath, ".data"));
+    format_hot_path_bash_command(
+        output_dir,
+        relpath,
+        format!(
+            "out={}; raw={}; tmp=$(mktemp); trap 'rm -f \"$tmp\"' EXIT; \
+             rm -f \"$raw\"; \
+             if perf mem record -o \"$raw\" -- {replay_command} >/dev/null 2>&1 && \
+                 perf mem report -i \"$raw\" --stdio > \"$tmp\" 2>&1; then \
+                 printf '%s\\n' 'capture=remote_access' 'selected=perf_mem' 'fallback=false' > \"$out\"; \
+                 cat \"$tmp\" >> \"$out\"; \
+             else \
+                 rm -f \"$raw\"; \
+                 printf '%s\\n' 'capture=remote_access' 'selected=cache_event_fallback' 'fallback=true' 'reason=perf mem unavailable or failed on this host' > \"$out\"; \
+                 perf stat -x, -e cache-references,cache-misses,page-faults -o \"$tmp\" -- {replay_command}; \
+                 cat \"$tmp\" >> \"$out\"; \
+             fi",
+            shell_escape(&output_path),
+            shell_escape(&raw_path),
+        ),
+    )
+}
+
